sections.label: Lyrics
lines: Wash my hands in the morning rain,
Let the old dust fall away.
No more fire built for show
Just a flame that lights the way.
Turn from the noise, from the hands that harm,
Find the face of a stranger to love.
Righteousness lives in open palms
Not the heights we’re dreaming of.
I hear the earth whisper soft and low,
The pulse of heaven begins to flow
Let justice roll like rivers wide,
And righteousness flow like a never-ending tide.
Break the dams we built with pride,
Let mercy flood this heart inside.
Let justice roll… let justice roll.
Feed the hungry, lift the weak,
Defend the ones who cannot speak.
Love without counting what it costs,
Find the found, and free the lost.
Don’t wear faith like borrowed clothes,
Truth is more than words you know.
It’s the river running through your soul,
A wild grace making broken whole.
When the system bends the poor to dust,
When the wounded lose their trust,
Let compassion be the louder sound,
Let our hands rebuild the ground.
Judge with kindness, walk with care,
Let your justice fill the air.
Not a sermon, but a life that moves
A living proof of love that proves.
Let the rivers rise where dry bones sleep,
In the silence, promise keeps.
Hear the cry from the streets below
Every tear becomes a seed that grows.
Let justice roll like rivers wide,
And righteousness flow like a never-ending tide.
No more walls, no hearts denied,
Heaven’s echo won’t be quiet.
Let justice roll… let justice roll.
justice roll…
